title: Daily Promo Spot — Color-Graded Key Frames, Thumbnail Pack, Cleaned VO & Sizzle Reel
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real brief: a daily promotional-video turnaround for a public-facing audience where the client supplies "fresh promotional footage" plus a voice-over via shared drive, wants "tasteful color correction," "the occasional caption or lower-third," "background music," a balanced/clean VO mix, a polished 1080p+ deliverable, and same-day consistency with a "lively and accessible…nothing overly corporate" style. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the brief's core ask (full Premiere timeline edit — trim/sequence/transitions/MP4 export + editable project) is NOT an Adobe-connector capability (video tools are async-widget [A]; video_resize is same-length-no-trim; quick_cut is AI-sizzle not timestamp trimming; no editable-project export, no music mixing). So I keep faithful to the brief's stated, connector-supported sub-tasks and reframe the DELIVERABLE as the same-day "promo package" a colorist/finisher actually hands back alongside the cut: a consistent color-grade applied to the client's exported key frames (matches category color_grade_lr and the brief's "tasteful color correction" + "lively, accessible, not corporate" look), a YouTube/social thumbnail derived from the hero frame (the brief's public-facing posting context), a royalty-free music-bed sourced from Adobe Stock (brief: "you can source royalty-free options"), a denoised voice-over (brief: "sync and balance the provided voice-over so it sits cleanly"), and a short engagement-cut sizzle + content summary for the post copy. The daily-folder return ("drop the finished files back in the folder") becomes the asset-folder packaging steps. No invented unrelated deliverable; every enrichment maps to a sentence in the brief.
one_line_ask: Take today's exported promo key frames + the provided voice-over, apply one consistent lively color grade across the frames, build a YouTube thumbnail from the hero shot, source a royalty-free music bed, clean the VO, generate a sizzle cut and a content summary, and drop the finished, organized package back in the daily folder — same day.
full_brief: Fresh promotional footage and a voice-over land in the shared drive each afternoon and the finished, post-ready package must go back in the same folder within 24 hours. For today's spot the client exported five representative key frames from the raw clips (hero close-up, two mid-shots, an environment/wide, and a product/detail shot), all shot in mixed afternoon lighting so they look uneven and slightly cool. They also dropped a raw voice-over WAV (room tone + a little hiss) and want a music bed sourced for them. Deliver a same-day "promo finishing package": (1) all five key frames carrying ONE consistent, lively-but-tasteful grade — auto-tone baseline, warmer temperature, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, a touch more vibrance, plus the same Lightroom look-preset on every frame so the set feels edited together and "accessible, not corporate"; (2) a 1920x1080 YouTube/social thumbnail built from the graded hero frame (subject popped from the busy background via a blurred-background treatment, then cropped to 16:9 with the subject weighted left so a title can sit right); (3) a licensed royalty-free music-bed candidate from Adobe Stock (full-res licensed file, upbeat/accessible); (4) the voice-over denoised so it sits cleanly in the mix; (5) a 15-second vertical-friendly sizzle cut from the source clips for the social teaser, plus a short auto-generated content summary to seed the post caption/description. Package the graded frames, thumbnail, licensed music, cleaned VO and sizzle into a dated daily-delivery folder in CC and confirm done. Keep the turnaround fast and the look consistent across every frame.

inputs.name: keyframe_hero_closeup.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal still frame exported from a promotional video: candid close-up of a mid-30s small-business owner laughing mid-sentence in a sunlit artisan coffee roastery, shot on a cinema camera at 1920x1080, mixed afternoon window light that reads slightly cool and uneven, real skin pores and flyaway hairs, shallow depth of field with a busy blurred shelf-and-equipment background, documentary not glossy, natural grain.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a frame-grab (slight motion-softness, 16:9 native, candid expression), not a posed studio portrait. Cool/uneven white balance is intentional so the grade has something to fix.

inputs.name: keyframe_mid_A.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: medium shot of the same coffee-roastery owner pouring beans from a scoop, mixed cool afternoon light, busy real-world background, candid documentary style, natural skin texture and grain, slightly underexposed shadows.
inputs.realism_notes: Same scene/subject and same cool, slightly-underexposed look as the hero so a single grade can unify all five frames.

inputs.name: keyframe_mid_B.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: medium shot of two staff members chatting over a roasting machine in the same coffee roastery, mixed cool window light, real textures, candid, natural grain, uneven exposure.
inputs.realism_notes: Different framing but identical lighting character; tests grade consistency across frames.

inputs.name: keyframe_wide_environment.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: wide environment shot of the artisan coffee roastery interior with customers, large windows blowing out slightly, cool afternoon cast, documentary, natural grain.
inputs.realism_notes: Brighter highlights here so the highlight-recovery step has work to do; same color cast for unity.

inputs.name: keyframe_product_detail.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: macro detail of a finished latte with rosetta art on a wooden counter in the same roastery, shallow depth of field, cool mixed light, real foam micro-texture, natural grain.
inputs.realism_notes: Detail/product shot completes the 5-frame set; same cool cast for consistent grading.

inputs.name: voiceover_raw.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: Warm, friendly female narrator, ~20 seconds, upbeat-accessible read: 'Every cup tells a story. Roasted fresh, poured with care, and shared with the people who make our mornings better. Come taste the difference this week.' Render then degrade slightly with steady room tone and faint tape hiss for an audio-repair test.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately left with hiss/room tone so media_enhance_speech has something to clean; voice-only (no music) per connector limits.

inputs.name: source_clips_for_sizzle.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p promotional b-roll montage of the same artisan coffee roastery: beans pouring, espresso pulling, latte art, owner laughing, customers chatting, warm natural light, handheld documentary feel, ~40 seconds of varied shots with native audio.
inputs.realism_notes: Longer multi-shot source so the AI sizzle cut has varied moments to pick from; 1080p native, not 720p/fast.

outputs.name: graded_keyframes/ (5 files)
outputs.spec: 5x 1920x1080 JPEG/PNG, each carrying the identical grade chain (auto-tone -> warmer temperature -> lifted shadows -> recovered highlights -> +vibrance -> same Lightroom look-preset), applied identically to all five frames (hero + 4). Visually consistent 'edited-together' lively look.

outputs.name: youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080.png
outputs.spec: 1920x1080 (16:9) PNG thumbnail from the graded hero frame: subject popped via the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset (subject stays sharp, busy background blurred), then cropped with subject weighted left, title-safe negative space on the right.

outputs.name: music_bed_licensed.{wav/mp3}
outputs.spec: Full-resolution LICENSED Adobe Stock royalty-free track (upbeat/accessible), delivered via presigned download URL after licensing.

outputs.name: voiceover_clean.wav
outputs.spec: Denoised/de-hissed version of the provided voice-over, voice-only, ready to balance into the mix.

outputs.name: sizzle_15s_teaser.mp4
outputs.spec: ~15s AI engagement-cut sizzle reel assembled from the source clips for the social teaser (async-widget result in product).

outputs.name: content_summary.txt
outputs.spec: Short auto-generated scene/transcript summary of the source montage to seed the post caption + description.

outputs.name: daily_delivery_YYYY-MM-DD/
outputs.spec: Dated CC asset folder containing all of the above, organized for same-day handback.

connector_workflow.note: Ingest the client-supplied files dropped in the shared drive: the 5 key frames, the raw voice-over WAV, and the source montage MP4. Initializes the upload session to CC storage.
Finalize the upload so every input has a stable CC asset id / URI to feed the chain (5 frames, VO, montage).
Start the grade spine on the hero frame: neutral auto-tone baseline to even out the uneven mixed-light exposure before the creative grade.
Push the cool afternoon cast warmer for the 'lively, accessible, not corporate' feel the brief asks for.
Lift the crushed shadows from the slightly-underexposed grab so detail returns without flattening contrast.
Pull back the blown window highlights so the brighter areas stay controlled — completes the tonal correction.
Add a touch of vibrance for the lively look while protecting skin tones.

Stamp the chosen Lightroom look-preset (the consistent 'house' look) on the hero. This same preset + the same temp/shadow/highlight/vibrance settings get repeated on the other four frames (steps 9-12) so the set reads as edited together. Produces graded frame 1 of 5.

Apply the exact same grade recipe established on the hero (auto-tone baseline + warmer temp + lifted shadows + recovered highlights + vibrance + the same Lightroom look-preset, batched into one adjustments call) to keyframe_mid_A so it matches. Graded frame 2 of 5.
Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_mid_B for a consistent 'edited-together' look. Graded frame 3 of 5.
Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_wide_environment (the blown-highlight frame benefits most from the shared highlight recovery). Graded frame 4 of 5.
Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_product_detail, completing the 5-frame graded set. Graded frame 5 of 5.
On the graded hero, select the subject (business owner) by prompt to build the mask that drives the subject-pop treatment.

Apply the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset: the preset uses the subject mask to keep the subject sharp while blurring the busy background, popping the subject for a clickable thumbnail. Consumes the graded hero + the subject mask (masked-preset path, not whole-image lens_blur).
Crop/resize the popped hero to exactly 1920x1080 (16:9) with the subject weighted left, leaving title-safe space on the right. Final thumbnail deliverable.
Search Adobe Stock (audio scope) for an upbeat, accessible royalty-free music bed matching the spot's friendly tone.
License the chosen candidate and pull the full-res file via presigned URL (license before use) — the royalty-free music bed deliverable.
Denoise/de-hiss the provided voice-over so it sits cleanly in the mix (voice-only, per connector limits). Async-widget in the product; consumes the ingested raw VO.
AI engagement-cut a ~15s social sizzle from the ingested source montage for the teaser. Async-widget; consumes the same ingested montage asset.
Summarize the source montage's scenes to seed the post caption/description copy. Async-widget; consumes the ingested montage.
Create the dated daily-delivery folder in CC storage for the same-day handback.
Copy all finished deliverables (all 5 graded frames, thumbnail, licensed music, cleaned VO, sizzle, summary) into the dated folder — the 'drop finished files back in the folder' handback step.

chaining_note: Single output->input spine with one shared ingest root. Steps 1-2 ingest the client files -> ingested_asset_ids. The color-grade spine runs strictly in series on the hero frame: ingested_asset_ids -> auto_tone -> temperature -> dark_portions -> highlights -> vibrance -> apply_preset = hero_graded_final (steps 3-8), each consuming the immediately prior output. The exact same recipe established on the hero is then applied to the other four frames via one batched image_apply_adjustments call each (steps 9-12, each consuming its own ingested frame) -> midA/midB/wide/product_graded_final, so all five frames are actually costed and consistent. The thumbnail branch consumes hero_graded_final: select_by_prompt builds hero_subject_mask (step 13), then image_apply_preset applies the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset consuming BOTH hero_graded_final + hero_subject_mask -> hero_subject_popped (step 14; masked-preset path, NOT whole-image lens_blur) -> crop_and_resize -> youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080 (step 15). The music branch chains asset_search -> license_and_download (16-17). media_enhance_speech (18), quick_cut (19), and summarize (20) each consume the shared ingested_asset_ids (VO and montage). Finally create_folders (21) -> copy_assets (22) consumes the folder plus every produced deliverable — all five graded frames, thumbnail, licensed music, cleaned VO, sizzle, summary — to assemble the dated handback. Every post-ingest step sets inputs_from to a prior step's named output.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 19 connector calls, all server=adobe, 19 distinct Adobe tools across four groups (image color/FX, asset/stock/storage, video, audio). It demands a real colorist decision chain (auto-tone baseline → temperature → shadow/highlight balance → vibrance → look-preset) held CONSISTENT across a 5-frame set, a masked subject-pop thumbnail (select_by_prompt → invert → masked lens_blur → 16:9 crop), licensed-stock sourcing (search→license→download in the correct order), voice repair, an AI sizzle cut, content summarization, and a packaged dated handback. It stays strictly inside connector limits: no Premiere timeline edit / project-file export, no trim-to-timestamp (video tools are tagged [A]; video_resize unused since it can't trim), no music mixing, image_fill_area avoided (solid-only), no generative fill / object removal / text-to-image / compositing — the only 'generative' touch (reframe-crop) is not used. Difficulty comes from sequencing a faithful, repeatable grade and respecting which sub-tasks are headless [C] vs async-widget [A].

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability used. image_crop_and_resize/reframe-crop avoided; no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. image_fill_area not used. Canvas never grown: thumbnail is a 16:9 CROP of the graded hero via image_crop_and_resize (no invented pixels). The lens_blur+mask contradiction is removed: background blur now uses the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset via image_apply_preset (whole-image lens_blur no longer given a mask). Stock is license-before-download (asset_search -> asset_license_and_download_stock). All 18 tools are real Adobe-connector tools.
reverify.notes: Both flagged issues fixed. (1) Doability: replaced steps 9-11 (select_by_prompt -> invert_selection -> masked lens_blur) with the documented masked-preset path — image_select_by_prompt builds the subject mask, then image_apply_preset applies the 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' masked Adaptive preset consuming the graded hero + subject mask; image_invert_selection and the masked image_apply_lens_blur are dropped. (2) Output under-representation: the 4 non-hero graded frames are now actually costed (4x image_apply_adjustments, steps 9-12) so the 'graded_keyframes/ (5 files)' output is real, and step 22 asset_copy_assets copies all five graded frames plus every other deliverable. tool_call_count updated 19->22; distinct_adobe_tools 19->18 (dropped invert_selection + lens_blur, added image_apply_adjustments); tools_used list reconciled. All async [A] steps remain correctly tagged and are product-doable per the sheet.
